mechanism: Decomposition reveals that current steepening is predominantly term-premium-driven rather than expectations-driven, enabling correct classification as bad steepening
regime_dependence: Required in all regimes; most critical when raw 2s10s gives ambiguous signals

mechanism: 30-50bp model divergence on 50-100bp estimates creates signal-to-noise approaching 1:1, undermining decomposition precision and propagating uncertainty to all downstream analysis
regime_dependence: Always present; most damaging when term premium is in the 50-100bp transitional zone

mechanism: 480bp combined r-star CI means the expected short rate component cannot determine whether policy is restrictive or accommodative, undermining the curve's theoretical recession mechanism
regime_dependence: Structural; worsens when r-star models diverge from each other and from market pricing

mechanism: $2T+ annual Treasury issuance creates persistent supply pressure on the long end, driving term premium higher and producing bear steepening without corresponding growth improvement
regime_dependence: Conditional on central bank non-intervention; nullified when Fed/other CBs absorb issuance via QE

mechanism: Suppresses measured term premium by 20-40bp in calm markets (synthetic QE) and amplifies spikes during stress, distorting both calm-state and stress-state decomposition readings
regime_dependence: Suppression channel operates in low-vol regimes; amplification channel activates during stress/deleveraging events

mechanism: Supply-driven term premium increases compress equity valuations without reflecting improved growth, exposing the inconsistency of low ERP under positive stock-bond correlation
regime_dependence: Operative under positive stock-bond correlation; attenuated under negative correlation where bonds hedge equity risk

mechanism: CLO arbitrage creates a binary credit supply channel: 50bp SOFR moves around the ~4.3-4.5% threshold produce regime changes in 65-70% of institutional leveraged loan funding
regime_dependence: Nonlinearity is most acute at SOFR 4.0-5.0%; functional below 3.5%, dead above 5.5%

mechanism: $1.7T in private credit with NAV smoothing (autocorrelation 0.5-0.7) masks stress for 12-18 months, degrading the yield curve's real-time information content about aggregate credit conditions
regime_dependence: Worsens as private credit share of total credit grows; untested through a full cycle at current scale

mechanism: Higher issuance raises term premium, increasing government interest costs, requiring yet more issuance—a self-reinforcing loop once the blended rate exceeds nominal GDP growth
regime_dependence: Activates when r-g gap closes and central bank does not intervene; US-specific threshold unknown; reserve currency premium may provide 200bp+ buffer

mechanism: BOJ normalization reduces carry incentive for Japan's ~$1.1T in UST holdings, reducing demand and amplifying supply pressure on term premium
regime_dependence: Operative when Fed-BOJ spread narrows enough to overcome institutional stickiness and hedging cost inertia

mechanism: MOVE levels help discriminate signal quality: <80 suggests expectations-driven curve moves (reliable), >120 suggests term-premium-contaminated moves (unreliable), though the diagnostic may be partially circular
regime_dependence: Most useful at extremes (<80 or >120); diagnostically ambiguous in the 80-120 middle zone where MOVE currently resides

mechanism: QE stock effects distort curve signals for 3-5 years after QE ends, with estimated 40-80bp residual distortion in 2026 reducing the curve's informational content
regime_dependence: Decays over 3-5 years post-QE; stronger immediately after large-scale QE programs

mechanism: Basis trade acts as synthetic QE by absorbing duration supply in calm markets, extending the effective QE distortion even after official QE has ended
regime_dependence: Operative while basis trade remains large-scale ($800B-$1T); could unwind rapidly during stress events

mechanism: Fed rate cuts (steepening trap trigger) lower SOFR but also precede rising defaults → CCC bucket expansion → CLO OC test failures → credit supply contraction—a specific causal chain through the CLO channel
regime_dependence: Operative when CLO arbitrage is in the marginal-viability zone (SOFR ~4.0-5.0%) and reinvestment periods are expiring

mechanism: Geopolitical escalation creates permanent sunk fiscal costs (defense industrial base, supply chain duplication) that persist after crises subside, structurally increasing fiscal issuance requirements
regime_dependence: Ratchet operates during escalation phases; could theoretically unwind over generational timescales (peace dividend)

mechanism: $250-350B in expiring reinvestment periods create asymmetric exposure: post-reinvestment CLOs cannot benefit from spread widening, amplifying the nonlinear credit supply response during stress
regime_dependence: Concentrated in 2025-2027 expiration window; magnitude unprecedented at current scale

mechanism: Central clearing mandate increases margin requirements, compressing leverage capacity of the basis trade and potentially triggering deleveraging that converts the suppression channel into the amplification channel
regime_dependence: Q2 2026 implementation; severity depends on phase-in speed and cross-margining offsets

mechanism: The bifurcation reflects that front end tracks cyclical policy expectations while back end reflects fiscal supply/term premium dynamics—supply-driven impact concentrates in the long end where positive equity correlation resides
regime_dependence: Emerged during term premium rebuilding regime; could collapse if term premium returns to negative territory or if inflation returns below 2%

mechanism: The current bear-steepening (bad steepening) regime is empirically untested for the steepening trap pattern; all historical precedents were bull-steepening episodes, creating a critical gap in applicability
regime_dependence: Key disagreement: may only apply to bull-steepening (front end falling on rate cuts), making current regime informationally distinct
